TextENGNew: "...Oh, Minai-san!@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1115. You're waking up?!"
She could make out a white ceiling, and the red light of the sunset pouring in through the window. And then she saw Fujita gazing at her with a relieved look on his face.
"...Where am I...?"
As soon as she got up, a dull pain resounded inside her head. She grimaced and took a look around at what appeared to be a hospital room.
She suddenly turned her gaze downward and noticed she was dressed in a nightgown. The straps around her chest were tied in a single half-knotted bow.
That was Madoka's signature style of knot. ...Which meant she must've been the one who changed her clothes.
"...Is Madoka here?"
"Huh?@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1117. Oh... she was here up until this afternoon."
"But the Chief insisted she must be tired from staying here all night, so he asked me to replace her."
"I see..."
"Oh, hold on a sec. Let me go call the doctor."
Fujita dashed out of the hospital room as soon as he finished saying that.
And a short while later, he returned alongside a doctor in a white coat and a nurse.
"How... how did I... get here...?"
"Take it easy, don't overdo it.@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1121a. Your injuries were nothing serious, but you've been asleep since last night."
"...Last night?"
While Tomoe quietly received a medical exam, memories revived in the back of her mind, causing her to gasp and take a look around.
This was a private room, and Fujita was the only other one here.
In which case... Oh no...?!
"Fujita-kun, what about Tsukada-san?!"
"Oh, he's all right.@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1123. You were brought here together, and his life isn't in any danger.@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1124. ...He should be sleeping in another room."
"...I see.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2530. Thank goodness."

When she heard that, Tomoe held a hand against her chest and laid her upper body down again in relief. ...Thanks to that and the comforting softness of the pillow, she spontaneously let out a deep sigh.
"...I see no signs of illness.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_docM1002. Seems you were just overworked."
Before long, the doctor finished the medical exam. He told Tomoe to "get some rest for now" and then exited the room along with the nurse. After seeing them go, Fujita began speaking in a whisper.

"The report said you were going on a date to visit the harbor as a couple.@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1126. According to Hayami-san from the mobile team, that was when the two of you fell in.@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1127. ...But what's really most important is that you're safe."
"...Sorry for making you worry."
"Hayami-san and his team were able to pull the car up from the seafloor."
"...You were really lucky.@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1130. Every door except the rear left-side was completely sealed shut. If you hadn't noticed it, you would've ended up at the bottom of the sea."
"Lucky, huh...?"
It wasn't luck... Tomoe muttered that to herself while gazing up at the ceiling, recalling the moment just before she escaped the car.
At the time... Tomoe was rapidly losing consciousness thanks to the alcohol injection, so she seriously thought it was all over.
But that moment, she was sure she heard a voice... Yes, it was Nagisa's voice.
"Minai-san, behind you!@k@vS14/35/VT4Bb_nagisa1076. The rear door!!"
If that voice hadn't called out to her, Tomoe would've been too busy focusing on the brakes to think of pushing on the door in the back.
(At any rate, why did I hear Nagisa-san's voice...?)
That hallucination she heard in her drunken stupor felt so realistic. And she'd never heard Nagisa talk like that before.
So, what on earth was that...?
"...Fujita-kun,@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2536. why was the rear left door the only one that could open?"

"Well, I was wondering about that, too...@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1132. Oh, but forensics is almost done on that car.@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1133. If we ask Hayami-san, maybe he'll be able to tell us something."
"Then, please.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2538. I know it's a hassle, but would you mind following up with Hayami-san?"
"Sure, I don't really mind, but...@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1135. is something the matter?"
"Just curious.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2540. ...Now then."
After saying that, Tomoe sat up and slipped her feet into the slippers on the floor. Then she strengthened her wavering consciousness and held a hand against the bed as she rose to her feet.
"Minai-san?@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1137. Wh-what are you...?!"
"...Step out of the room for a sec, I'm going to get changed.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2542. My suit is... ah, here it is."

"Y-you can't do that!@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1139. You were comatose until just a few minutes ago, you know?@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1140. You should follow the doctor's orders and get some rest, at least for today...!"
"This is a critical moment.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2544. ...Sorry, I realize this is selfish."
"Minai-san..."
But Tomoe continued dragging her clearly exhausted body, reaching for her suit on the wall. Then she slapped both her cheeks with her hands to wake herself up, and raised her head.
(Should I try to stop her?@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1143. Nah...)
Fujita seemed to come to an understanding while looking at Tomoe's tense expression and watching the whole scene unfold. He kept quiet and left the hospital room.
No matter what happened, nobody could stop her now. ...After realizing that, he decided to walk down the hall, thinking about how he might get discharge permission from the doctor.
Madoka was walking down the hallway, but when she saw her sister emerge from the entrance, she was so startled she nearly dropped the papers she had in hand.
She sleeplessly staying by Tomoe's side until just a little while ago.

She refused Fujita's concerned requests to trade place with her no matter how many times he asked. And even after Yamaoki ordered her to come back to the station, only a few hours had passed since she reluctantly acquiesced.
(...I'll head out early today and stay overnight in Sis's hospital room.)
Though she was walking and thinking that, her sister was right there in front of her, standing with Fujita...?
Unbelievable, Madoka thought as she rushed over to Tomoe.
"A-are you all right?@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1076. Sis, you were sound asleep for so long, so I... I...!"
"I'm fine.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2546. The bleeding has stopped, and it won't leave any scars."
Tomoe wore a courageous smile as she said that, lifting her bangs to reveal the bandage applied there.
Her injuries were mild, just as Fujita said. ...Even so, her face still looked pale even in the light of the sunset, so she didn't seem healthy by any stretch.
Madoka's eyes were open wide as she struggled to find the words to respond.
But then she noticed Fujita standing beside Tomoe, shaking his head at her... and decided to keep her mouth shut.
Madoka had already heard from Fujita and Yamaoki about the identity of the "passenger" taken to the hospital along with her.
So there was probably no need to ask Tomoe why she was forcing herself back to work...
"No need to worry.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2548. You can get back to your usual duties... See you later."
"Ah, Sis, wait!"
Just as Tomoe was about to turn and leave, Madoka called out to her in a strong tone, so Tomoe looked back over her shoulder to face her.
And Madoka thought about whether or not to say anything... before deciding to tell her about the visitor who just came to the Chief's office.
"Kaoru-sa... I mean, Chief Yamaoki currently has a guest.@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1080. And I heard it was the Director of the NPA Public Relations Office."
"...Maeno-san?"
"...Excuse me, it's Minai."
"Come in."
Hearing that response from her light knock, Tomoe opened the door and stepped into the Chief's office.
Yamaoki was in the room, and Maeno was sitting on the sofa across from him, waiting for her.
"Director Maeno...?"
"Hey, Princess.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1094. I hear you're achieving great results."
Maeno was sitting with his back toward her, and turned his head to smile at Tomoe.
Realizing he was saying that sarcastically, she didn't bother to argue and just said "...I'm sorry" while deeply bowing.
"How are you feeling?"
"...Absolutely awful."
That differed from the answer she gave Madoka, but it wasn't Tomoe's wounds she was referring to.

Fortunately, she was able to escape from the car before it plunged into the sea, but she was sleeping for a long time in the hospital... And Tsukada, who was riding along with her, still hadn't woken up.
And according to Fujita, they didn't know the whereabouts of the perp who tried to murder them, Hanada. All their effort was in vain.
"An elite female assistant manager, acting on her own without informing her superiors, getting involved in vehicular action like something out of a Hollywood movie?"
"...That sort of heroics is not the norm here.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1097. Are you aiming to be an actor instead of a police officer?"
"...I'm sorry."
The biting implications behind Maeno's words... was painful.

She made contact with people related to the case without informing investigation HQ, then fell into danger. Plus, a key reference person ended up hospitalized, making it impossible to question him for the time being.
...It was a major blunder.
"...Anyway, there's a problem."
Maeno picked up the tabloid sitting on the desk and handed it to Tomoe.
She opened to the page marked with a sticky note, and as soon as she read the headline, she cried out with her eyes open wide in astonishment.
"Wh-what... i-is the meaning of this?!"

"Exactly what's written there.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1100. You and Congressman Chiba's secretary were involved in a secret love affair, but once it was broken off he tried to force both of you into double suicide.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1101. ...It's the sort of love-hate drama that housewives totally eat up."

Her thoughts were aghast as she read the text of the article. ...It detailed Tomoe and Tsukada's high school friendship, their junior/senior relationship at the time, and even the rumors that they were dating.
(There's even testimony from an old acquaintance... How is that possible?!)
The conniving content of the article made her want to tear it to shreds. It was as if they were prepared in advance for her to get involved in this scandal...?!

"Assistant Manager Minai.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1103. You're free to get involved in love life as you see fit... but if you intend to get ahead in this world you really need think carefully about the men you choose, you know?"
"And the kind of man who'd force a suicide pact on you the moment you mention breaking up is nothing good for you."
"And depending on the other party's profession, it could lead to false suspicion.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1106. ...Please be careful in the future."
"N-no... it's not like that!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2559. Tsukada-san and I weren't..."

"At times like these, the truth is of little consequence.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1108. As far as the masses are concerned, everything they care about is written here.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1109. ...Well fortunately it wasn't an adultery scandal, so the world may yet sympathize with you."
Tomoe bit her lip as she was reminded of the circumstances that got her caught in this unexpected trap.
If the enemy's goal was to cut off her activities, she should've been more careful and prepared for the possibility they'd aim for her life.
But there were other more effective ways to take her down, as well. And that would be to undermine Tomoe's social status and discredit her honor...!
(So, this was a two-step attack... Damn it!)
Even if Tomoe's assassination failed, she'd be ostracized by society thanks to this subsequent farce... or her value to society would be greatly diminished.
Such a carefully laid trap was nothing short of a superhuman feat.
"For the time being, it's hard to say if it'll become a major scandal... but since it made its way to the mass media punishment of some form is definitely in order."
"I'll have to ask you to step away from your press position here at joint investigation HQ.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1112. Please rest quietly at home until formal disciplinary action has been decided."
"...M-Maeno-san!"
"Why so gloomy, Minai-kun?"

"Your body must be worn out from coming into work every single day for such a long time, right?@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1214. So take this as an opportunity to get a little rest.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1215. Maintaining a healthy mind and body is one of the necessary duties of a police officer."
"Rest?!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2564. Not a chance...!"
She didn't need comfort, and she definitely didn't need rest. Because there was something she wanted to do. Something she had to do...!
"...@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1185.Hey, Minai.@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1186. I've finally decided... I'm going to try believing in fate."
After Tsukada said that, he trusted her... and revealed his secret to her.
That trust could only be repaid by taking charge of this investigation and arresting the criminals that put them in this situation.
In a sense, Tomoe had to answer his trust by demonstrating that there was still justice in this world...!
"Tsukada-san... Osamu Tsukada told me something critical!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2566. The man behind the incident I'm pursuing is none other than Congressman Akihiko Chiba!"
"And he also told me that dangerous human experiments are being conducted with drugs developed by Lowell Inc!"
"And Hanada told me!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2569. My father was investigating the secret machinations of these politicians, and that was the reason he and my mother were murdered!!"
"Tomoe-kun..."
"Hoh?@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1114. Then... you want to clear up your parents' lingering regrets?@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1115. Is that what you're saying?"
"That's not it!!"
Tomoe leaned toward the two of them with enough momentum it was like she was going to snap at them.
She didn't intend to pursue the criminal for personal hatred or resentment. That would simply be selfish.
She had a stronger... more important goal. She wanted to fulfill justice... No, she had to!
"Tsukada-san told me!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2572. He said... this is for vengeance!"
"He knew about what happened to my parents!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2574. He saw how much it tore me and my sister apart, and he sympathized with us!"
It's true that he may not have been able to physically do anything for them.
But for Tomoe, who was so hurt by the insatiable curiosity of the mass media that she saw everyone as an enemy, Tsukada's sympathy was just as precious as her chance encounter with Ooishi.
"And... for us, for our sake, he did some dirty work and sacrificed years of his life in order to learn the true identity of our enemy. He did it all to help us!"
"So, I have a duty to repay that trust!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2577. If I can't repay that debt, I will have failed in that duty!"
"What's that...? You're talking like the yakuza.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1117. Vengeance, duty...? You can't measure the weight of those on your own, you moron."
"If you let that sentimentalism creep into your thoughts, your idea of justice is nothing more than self-satisfaction. How many times do I have to tell you that before you'll understand?"
"Calm down, Tomoe-kun.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1218. You won't accomplish anything unless you can stay cool."

After that scornful rejection from Maeno and Yamaoki's pleading... Tomoe released the hand that had been tightly gripping the back of the sofa... and took several deep breaths to get her breathing under control.
Upon seeing that, Maeno stirred the cup of tea in front of him, took a sip to moisten his throat, and then continued.
"...Chief Inspector Minai.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1120. You're interpreting this as a splash of cold water on your face... but I believe there's something you misunderstood."

"There are certain circumstances with regards to Osamu Tsukada.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1122. It seems he was dating a woman back when he was in college.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1123. I hear he had planned to introduce her to his parents and get married after they graduated."
"Had... planned to?"
Tomoe's eyes opened wide after hearing something she couldn't have imagined.
She knew there was another woman in Tsukada's life from that time she noticed him wearing a wedding ring.
In fact... maybe it was female intuition talking, but she felt something in the atmosphere last night when they were eating and when they were walking together.
But... planned to? Which meant he never actually got married?
(Come to think of it...)
Maeno did mention earlier that Tomoe and Tsukada's relationship was "not an affair." In other words, Tsukada had to be single.
Then... what on earth happened to this other woman...?
"The woman's name was Masumi Ounuma.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1220. The only daughter of Shigeru Ounuma."
"Masumi... Ounuma...?"
"You probably don't know her... but I'm sure you've heard of her father."

"Shigeru Ounuma, assistant director of the Pharmaceutical Safety Bureau at the Ministry of Health and Welfare.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1223. ...One of the bureaucrats arrested nine years ago during the Lowell investigation, on the suspicion of conspiring with other political and financial officers."
Tsukada first met Masumi Ounuma at a political research club in college. The fact that they were in the same grade and enrolled in the same department helped bring them together.
But, at that time... just like in high school, Tsukada had an extremely divisive reputation.

He was smart, assertive... and not bad to look at. But he mercilessly looked down on those he thought didn't put in enough effort or didn't take their studies seriously, so he wasn't an easy person to get along with.
Thus, many people shunned him for that arrogance, who in turn slandered him, claiming that he only approached Masumi for her father's political connections and his ambition to rise in the world.

"Masumi thought that too and seemed wary at first, not looking for a deep relationship.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1125. After all, she was studying hard aiming to become a government official just like her father."
Still, Tsukada was serious. And just like during his time with Tomoe, he was very reliable and had a deep affection for people who truly tried their hardest.
That sincerity eventually won Masumi over, and the two of them became so close their relationship was famous on campus... And they vowed to get married as soon as they graduated.
But the two of them ultimately had to separate... shortly after Ounuma was arrested with ties to the Lowell Incident.

"Ounuma was arrested thanks to the Lowell scandal and later died in prison.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1127. He allegedly had some pre-existing form of organ disease, but the truth of the matter is still unknown.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1128. And his daughter was so hurt by the rumors... that she felt cornered and committed suicide at home via gas inhalation."

"A lot of criticism was levied toward her thanks to their father/daughter relationship, and the degree of their attacks was apparently quite terrible.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1130. ...She allegedly died in her sleep, dressed in the wedding dress she was planning to wear that following year."
Killing yourself in a wedding dress...
It showed just how deep her love for Tsukada was... and it's easy to imagine that taking away the source of her happiness caused her to hate everything.
She must've talked about it with Tsukada a lot. And he must've offered her all the encouragement he could, trying to cheer her up.
But... even those feelings couldn't mend her broken heart.
And knowing that fact must've driven him to despair, and hatred...
"Don't gloss over it with lip service!@k@vS14/33/VT4Bb_tsukada1188. You don't know anything!"
She now understood the remorse and regret that fueled those vitriolic words back then.

And knowing that no matter how desperately you fought, no matter how much you bet your life on it... the despair of realizing that someone dear to you would never come back... was something she herself had experienced.
"Still... this Tsukada guy has balls of steel."

"Back in college, his acquaintances said... he had a very calculating personality, deciding who to date based on how much use they had to him.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1133. ...And yet, he threw everything away to fight for the one he loved?"
"Was he really that passionate a guy?"
"...Yes.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2586. Criticism and misunderstandings made him a lot of enemies... but he was a man with strong beliefs and ideals."
Madoka never thought well of him... but the Tsukada that Tomoe knew was a very kind person.

But because he was so devoted to his ideals and beliefs, he was very hard on himself and others, and treated people coldly when he disagreed with their values. It was sure to give an off-putting impression.

...He was very talented. So he failed to recognize the existence of less talented individuals, and he really believed that everyone could achieve the results they deserved as long as they put in the necessary effort.
He was a very pure and, in a sense, unfortunate person...
(...Even so.)
He was always a kind person, making great effort to look out for people like Tomoe who continued to work hard at things like track and field.
So, maybe that was the reason Masumi Ounuma was drawn to him... why she loved him...
(Dedicating a lifetime of work for the sake of one woman, huh...?)
It didn't seem like the Tsukada who had high ambitions, and yet did seem very like him at the same time.
He wasn't a man to cut corners at anything. Especially not someone he cared deeply about.
He was probably wearing that wedding ring to show his sincere desire to remember her even after her death.
She felt a hint of regret that she wasn't the target of that affection... but that felt a little selfish and greedy of her.
For Tsukada... and for Tomoe too, they were just a thing of the past...
"At any rate... you'll be on administrative leave for a while.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1136. Please understand, these circumstances leave no room for debate."
"Right..."

"Well... it's not all bad news.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1138. Peddling unlicensed drugs without patient consent... and what's worse, a major hospital dealing with something explicitly prohibited, is something completely unforgivable."
"So, joint investigation HQ is currently increasing staff and planning to continue the investigation.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1140. ...I'll be sure to appoint someone you can trust as your successor."
"...@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2592.It was selfish of me to want to be involved until the very end, after all..."

"I understand how you feel.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1142. ...But for now, get some rest, Chief Inspector Minai.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1143. There are so many things I'd like you to work on going forward.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1144. Can you be satisfied leaving things how they are here?"
"You did a fine job here.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1146. Yamaoki-san and I are both abundantly aware of that."
After saying that, Maeno got up from the sofa and gave Tomoe a stern pat on her drooping shoulders.
She timidly looked up to see Maeno nodding at her, not with his usual slanted smile, but with a strong, sincere, fearless smile.

"Our enemy is on the scale of cabinet ministers.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1148. It's impossible to stand up to that at the scope we've been assuming thus far.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1149. You've done more than enough to give us a solid foundation to work from.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1150. ...So back away peacefully and trust us to handle the rest.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1151. All right?"
"...@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2595.Right."
Tomoe answered affirmatively, then gave him a salute and left the room. ...And then, as soon as the tension dissipated, she felt the fatigue course all through her shoulders.
Madoka had been waiting at the front of the Chief's office, and she immediately caught her sister when she saw her staggering and wobbling out of the room.
She nearly collapsed from the momentum, but thankfully Fujita was next to them and able to hold her up.
"M-Minai-san?!"
"H-hold on?!@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1083. Hang in there, Sis...@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1084. Eh?"
"Wh-what's wrong?"
"...@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1086.She fell asleep..."
Just as Madoka had said... Tomoe had fallen into a deep slumber, while still standing up.
After seeing Tomoe off, Maeno turned toward Yamaoki and shrugged his shoulders.
"Handling it like that... was the right thing to do, wasn't it?"
"I'm sorry...@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1225. I had you come all the way out here, and even made you play the part of the villain."
"...Well, it may still not be enough, but at least we pushed on the brakes a little bit.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1154. I don't want to lose an excellent subordinate to this."
Maeno sat down on the sofa as he said that, and offered Yamaoki a cigarette. He pulled out a lighter and lit it, then lit another one for himself.
"But it usually takes at least a day before written articles make their way here.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1227. Media operations are certainly your strong suit."

"I suppose.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1156. But by pulling the princess off the investigation through disciplinary action, at least we've removed any reason for them to kill her.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1157. I'd hate for them to get carried away and launch a counterattack."
"Besides, a scandal like this really won't be bad for her career."
Maeno grinned and had a sharp challenging glint in his eyes as he adjusted his glasses.
...Tomoe herself was unaware of this, but the NPA Public Relations Office also had a "shadow intelligence department" under Maeno's command that went all the way up to the Commissioner's Secretariat.
Considering the social influences on criminal investigation, controlling the information released to the public can be an effective deterrent against would-be criminals and underground organizations.

This is possible because the position of media information management authority was directly delegated by the Director of the National Police Agency. Until now they've been able to halt the flow of information with a single request, or vice versa.
In that sense, it is very easy to disseminate rumor-level information through the press and portray it as the truth, which made them an even more powerful police organization than Public Security.
"And... she'd be furious if she knew the truth, but this incident also gave us a perfect justification to get a search warrant for the Tsukada residence."

"Thanks to that, we were able to salvage some of the secret ledgers he was managing for Congressman Chiba.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1161. Well, the rest is in the hands of the special investigation department at the District Prosecutor's Office."
"Can we expect this to succeed?"
"Honestly, I give it about 50/50 odds that the Nagoya District Prosecutor will pull through.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1163. Not terrible, but hardly decisive."

"And even if so, they might simply dismiss it as something the secretary did on his own.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1165. Who knows if that would be enough to pursue a parliamentary inquiry?@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1166. And the general election is coming up soon, so there's a good chance they'll opt not to do anything about it."
The contents of the secret ledgers they seized were at best documenting donations and campaign expenses. Certainly proof of illegal dealings, but not something particularly unusual for a politician.

Ideally, they wanted to find something showing him acting as an intermediary with relevant businesses and government offices during his days as a cabinet minister, but unfortunately that wasn't the case.
"Chiba's first secretary Koyama died of acute heart failure last year...@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1168. And after that, Tsukada took ownership of all of his documents."
"Which means there should be other documents in his possession that we haven't discovered yet.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1170. ...Investigation is still ongoing to determine where they might be hidden."
"However, I'm aware that police are limited in what they can do on matters related to corruption."

"The real question is, will the District Prosecutor's Office be willing to take this seriously and act accordingly?@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1230. If the people betrayed in the Lowell scandal escape the pressure from above and stand up, they might have a chance at success."
"Hmm... That'd be difficult.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1173. Chiba is even more influential now than he was back then, and he even has connections in the upper ranks of the police."

"Back during the Lowell Incident, there was a foreign conspiracy at play so Public Security took their side in defense of national interests. But this time, they'd likely side with preserving the government in office."
"From my background there, I think it's easy to imagine.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1176. ...That goes the same for you, right, Yamaoki-san?"
"Plus, the district attorney was an underclassman of Secretary Okuno.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1232. ...Quite a predicament."
This incident was likely to be pinned on another scapegoat. A medical institution will be caught distributing prohibited drugs without patient consent, and the people involved will be arrested.
It might be a serious blow for Okuno and Chiba's Alphabet Project, but at least it would control the spread of information and keep the details of the research secret.
And then the truth will be buried in darkness, as Chiba runs away with huge sums of cash lining his pockets...
Yamaoki couldn't help but feel remorse, thinking it was hopeless to do anything against Koizumi's flunkies.
"...But I won't let them escape.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1178. I'll do everything in my power to make it happen.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1179. I'll teach these foreign-financed jackasses a lesson."
"Maeno-kun...?"

"Sure, the District Prosecutor's Office may give up before reaching Chiba.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1181. And I heard from Akiyama in Public Security, they're doing as much as they can to push responsibility onto that old-timer who has already retired and no longer has any authority to set one hell of an example."
"...But I won't allow these merchants of death to profit any longer.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1183. No matter how many years it's been, I'll cut down these diplomatic ties to Lowell... I swear it."
"...Is it all right to be so enthusiastic?@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1235. You don't hear a lot of cases about someone picking a fight with a politician and coming out unscathed, you know?"
"Hahah! So what?@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1185. If it's something you can do, you might as well try.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1186. But unlike the Princess, I'll receive my recompense for it."
Maeno's eyes were heating up and turning red. But when Yamaoki started feeling suspicious of his violent reaction... he suddenly spoke up.
"You can laugh if you want... but I think this is fate."
"Fate...?"

"That Minai-san's daughter would be the one to crack the mystery he was pursuing.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1189. If destiny is real, I can't think of anything more appropriate.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1190. So, I'm going to devote my whole career to this."
"Besides, I'm living carefree as a bachelor.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1192. After this is all over, I think it'd be nice to spend the rest of my life out in the country."
"...I see."
Yamaoki completely understood his enthusiasm. Because for Maeno, Tomoe's father... Yuusuke Minai was his first boss and a mentor.

That hierarchy was quickly reversed thanks to the nature of the career group... but he looked up to Minai so much that he continued speaking to him in utmost respectful tone even after attaining a higher rank than him.
They were a perfect match for one another. After all, Maeno had an especially prideful and irreverent personality back then... but he always calmly listened to what Minai had to say.
But then...
"I still remember the Shirakaba mountain villa.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1194. One of the criminals had shot me in the leg during the raid, and I was certain his next shot would be in my head."
"If Minai-san hadn't been prepared for disciplinary dismissal and willing to ignore no-fire orders... I think I'd have been promoted to my current rank as they put me in the grave."
Maeno had a distant look in his eyes as he stamped his cigarette butt down on the ashtray. When Yamaoki squinted his eyes, Maeno's form overlapped with that of another person.
Twelve years had passed... since Yuusuke Minai's death. The two of them were getting up there in age. Their ranks had risen and they'd transferred to different workplaces.
But they had one thing in common. ...The remorse they felt back then, when upper management forced them to abandon an investigation even though their friend was murdered.
"Sorry, Minai-san!@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1197. Sorry, sorry...!!"
The sound of Maeno kneeling down in front of Yuusuke Minai's grave, spilling tears while apologizing nonstop... still lingered in Yamaoki's ears.
Yamaoki himself wanted to cry just as much as Maeno. But as the head of an organization, he felt an obligation to maintain a minimum level of dignity.
He may have even envied Maeno's position at the time, for being able to release his passion as he saw fit...
"Then... you're seeking revenge?@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1239. You too, Maeno-kun?"

"Revenge? ...No, not quite.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1199. If anything, I'd say it's proof of my pride as part of the career group.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1200. And my selfish desire to fulfill that purpose... is what allowed me to climb the ladder and gain power."
Maeno shrugged his shoulders, then bitterly smiled as he continued.
"Yamaoki-san.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1202. Back in the old days, you told me:@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1203. If you want to make the justice you believe in real, achieve greatness."
"No matter how correct or convincing your argument may be, the higher-ups in Japanese society will hardly bother to listen to someone with no title and no proven track record... Isn't that right?"
"Yes... I remember that."
It was back when he was on the team pursuing the truth of the Lowell Incident. When orders came down from upper management to suspend the investigation.
Maeno was the youngest among them and the most passionate about arresting the culprit who killed his old boss, and shortly afterwards he was forced to transfer out of Public Security.

"If you hadn't told me that back then, I would have quit the police force.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1206. Maybe I would've turned to social justice in desperation, and joined up with some anti-establishment movement."
"In that sense, you were always a very genuine person.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1242. And even now, I don't think all that much has changed."

"Hahahah.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1208. But thanks to your guidance, I was able to expand the power of the Public Relations Office in all sorts of ways.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1209. So I'm deeply grateful."
"...But, Yamaoki-san, lately I've started to think there might be another role for me to play, different from what you told me."
"We don't achieve greatness in order to realize our own personal justice."
"We're in our current positions... so the young people who believe in their stupidly honest ideas of justice can do crazy things for the sake of those beliefs."

"We elders are like the rungs on a ladder.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1214. Until I came here, I was being supported and protected by elders like you, and that's what allowed me to achieve greatness.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1215. Now it's my turn to play that role."
"...Maeno."
"Lending support behind the scenes... and when things get rough, holding out your neck and accepting responsibility so they don't have to."
"By doing that, they can continue freely believing in justice, and sometimes even take on risks in order to confront evil."
"I see."

"Regardless of the facts... we mustn't doubt our subordinates.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1219. It's the duty of the police to achieve their idea of justice. We can't be giving them the idea they might be able to distort that for someone's convenience."
"They risk their lives, believing their actions are in the best interest of the nation, the people."
"But if they're running around in confusion while we're in a position to show them the way, how can we say we're carrying out the duties of the police with responsibility and conviction?"
Police bureaucrats are like officers in the military. They send detectives to the scene of dangerous incidents and make every effort to apprehend the criminals.
That comes with its fair share of bloodshed. Sometimes lives are even lost.
Nevertheless, the upper ranks legitimize their actions and give them pride in their contributions: their own brand of justice.
Maeno believed that his role was to give those soldiers the boldness and resolve to follow them.
"But... Chiba is a big name in the major ruling party.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1247. Next time you'll be the target of attack."

"No need to worry about that.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1223. The House of Representatives election is coming up soon, so I'm speaking with some of the superiors in the opposition party to get some obstacles out of the way.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1224. It's good material for them to attack the ruling party."

"And even in the majority party, most people remember the fear of the ruling party falling apart 13 years ago.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1226. So, if we can gather some more damning material and make it stick, they'll even consider cutting Chiba out of the party."
"To do that... they'll need another push."

"Yes. Rest assured, I'll be taking care of investigation HQ for the time being.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1228. The Gifu prefectural police are also going to be pressured by the higher-ups in the office of the Commissioner's Secretariat.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1229. ...I'll try to make contact with the Imamura faction, as well."
"The Imamura faction...? As in Youhei Imamura?"
Hearing Maeno speak such an unexpected name, Yamaoki looked back at him with a sharp gaze.
Youhei Imamura. ...The former Minister of Foreign Affairs, and head of the current opposition faction in politics.
He was suspected of involvement in the Lowell scandal nine years ago because his son-in-law was one of the bureaucrats arrested... so at the moment, he had lost some power.

"By getting them involved, it's a great opportunity to overthrow Okuno and Chiba who wield the greatest power in the current government.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1231. I bet they'll bite at this bait... No, I can guarantee they'll be interested."

"I'm not too keen on doing that faction a favor... but the enemy of my enemy is my friend.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1233. We have to cut Chiba off before he gets surrounded by the entire ruling party...@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1234. We've come this far, so I can't be picky about the means."

"...Understood.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1251. Still, to think that one of the people who suffered most from the Lowell scandal would be launching a counterattack against the enemy with the same story.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1252. ...That's so far beyond irony it has turned into a comedy."

"Indeed.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1236. As far as they're concerned, it's a battle of revenge over their precious disciples.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1237. And what's more, it's a great opportunity to restore their former power..."
"They say that fate exacts painful revenge on those with evil intentions... but I never expected it to be exactly that."
"Fate, huh...?"
As Yamaoki muttered that to himself, he suddenly had a thought.
He had always believed that fate was the will of the gods... In a sense, he saw it as something beyond human ability.
But he finally realized that was wrong. ...No, it was demonstrated to him.
Yes... destiny can be moved and reshaped by the hands of human will.
And without a doubt, the reason he was seeing this current development... was because of Tomoe Minai's strong beliefs, and her courage to never give up...
(I was trying to make her wish come true... but maybe things played out the other way around.)
With a bitter smile, a feeling of happiness and a sense that he was not alone floated to his face.
A weak, unreliable baby bird had grown into a woman strong enough to inherit her father's will.
And she's been energizing Maeno and the other men around her, inspiring them to tackle the challenges before them.
Yamaoki had once given up on it... but the ideal form was certainly there.
"Well, anyway..."
"Hmm?@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1256. Something worrying you?"
"Ah, no.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1241. Doujima-san at investigation HQ understand the situation, so that's part is okay... but the problem is Oouchi."

"Thanks to a personnel exchange between NRIPS and certain government agencies, he's been temporarily transferred from the Ministry of Health and Welfare.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1243. And unlike Souma, he's a faint-hearted person who feels a strong need to succeed.@k@vS14/00/VT4Bb_maeno1244. Maybe I'm just overthinking something trivial."
That anxiety would eventually come to fruition in a very unexpected manner.
"...Hey, you're still on leave for another week, aren't you?@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1088. I've said this so many times, but I'll say it again... Don't do anything stupid!"
"Oh, shut up!"
Tomoe discontentedly uttered that as Madoka carefully helped her stuff investigation materials from one of the First Division's desks into a cardboard box.
She thought about smacking her across the head with one of the files in hand, but decided against it because Fujita was quietly organizing materials nearby (afraid, and from a safe distance).

"Sheesh... No matter how much previous success you've had, if you get punished too much you're looking at a salary reduction, you know?@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1090. Think of your bonuses, your pension...@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1091. Don't people around your age start looking to become homeowners?"
"I'm fine!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2599. I don't plan to stay in Tokyo or Kakiuchi for a long time anyway.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2600. If anything happens, I can always move back to Ibaraki."

"Ah... Sis, didn't you hear?@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1093. Grandma's old house is going to be demolished next year to make room for a parking lot.@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1094. She said she's going to use the money she saved up to live in a rental apartment."
"Huuh?!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2602. What?! I haven't heard any of that!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2603. She should at least try to renovate the place!"

"Hmm, she said she found a man she likes, so she wants to move in with him.@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1096. It's amazing~, finding new love even in her 80s.@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1097. ...Maybe you could learn a thing or two about that?"
With no suitable retort, Tomoe took the file in her hands and slammed it down inside the cardboard box.
Then she sealed it with duct tape, set up another box, and resumed filling it.
"Still though, getting into a scandal before finding a boyfriend...@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1099. That sounds just like you, huh, Sis?@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1100. Don't you think so too, Fujita-san?"
"Huh?@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1147. N-no..."
Don't drag me into this, Fujita muttered to himself. But Tomoe glanced at him and suddenly felt something wicked, then spoke up.

"In that case, maybe I should tell a little something to the next newspaper reporter that interviews me...@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2606. "There's someone I like.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2607. He's a subordinate, but he's the same age as me...""

"Eeeek?!@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1149. S-stop right there, Minai-san!!@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1150. If such a bald-faced lie made its way into a newspaper article, you know Yuko would straight up murder meeee!!"
"...I'm kidding.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2609. There's no need to get so worked up."
Of course, she had no romantic feelings for Fujita, but it would be completely boring to outright reject the notion.

...That being said, his wife was famous all across the police station for being a jealous monster, and Tomoe herself had been on the receiving end of that fury once before, so she understood that particular aspect of his dread.
"A-anyway!@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1152. Minai-san, Madoka-san, are either of you thirsty?@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1153. I'll g-go buy you something to drink!"
Then, Fujita dashed out of the room like a startled rabbit, without even waiting for a reply. ...It happened so fast, Tomoe and Madoka were left stunned.
"...Hehe."
"Ahaha..."
The two of them looked at one another, then burst out laughing out of nowhere. And then,
"But yeah... Sis, you're incredible."
"Huh?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2613. What are you talking about...?"
Thinking that was a sarcastic provocation, Tomoe turned and glared at Madoka. ...But she had a calm, gentle smile on her face as she gazed at Tomoe, and then continued.
"Everyone else had given up on this case, saying it was impossible to find the truth, but you finally reached it."
"Everyone at joint investigation HQ had nothing but praise to say about you.@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1106. They said it was wrong to judge someone just because she's young, or a woman."
"And this might be a strange thing to say now, but... I never really wanted to become a police officer."

"To be honest, I just couldn't pour everything I had into investigating Mom and Dad's case like you did.@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1109. ...And I always thought it was a dangerous, unrewarding job.@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1110. But..."
One day, while Madoka was working at the cabaret club, Yamaoki happened to visit as a customer... He looked at Madoka's face and asked her a sincere question.
"Have we... met somewhere in the past?"
"Ahaha, what are you talking about?@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1112. That sort of pickup line went out of fashion ages ago, didn't it?"
She wore a business smile on her face, while sighing deep in her heart.
She sick of dirty middle-aged men using those sorts of lines. She'd like to think that she had a little more taste than that.
...However.
"Oh, sorry... there's a girl I'm looking after that looks a lot like you.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1259. Her name is Tomoe Minai... Do you know her?"
When Madoka heard that, she immediately dropped the muddler she was using to stir the whiskey.
She ran away from her sister to avoid being such a bother for her... plus, even when Tomoe came looking for her, Madoka made a declaration that she was on her own, and turned away.
Nevertheless... Madoka still hadn't forgotten her sister.
Considering how many years it's been, she had probably graduated and gotten a job around now. But was she still living a depressing life, being beaten down day by day?
In the long stretch of time they hadn't seen one another, only bad thoughts ran through her head, sometimes even creeping into her dreams...
With that in mind, Madoka completely forgot about her job and then leaned forward.
"You know my sister?@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1115. ...I'm her little sister, Madoka."
"I see... I thought so.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1261. I knew that Yuusuke had two daughters... So you're the younger one?"
Surprisingly enough, Yamaoki also acquainted with Madoka's parents... In fact, he was her father's boss.
So that meant he was a fellow police officer...?
"So, uhh... how is Sis doing?@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1117. I don't really know what's going on with her these days..."
"Yeah... she's doing well.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1263. She's currently working for Ibaraki prefectural police, in the youth division. She has a good reputation with her colleagues and superiors."
"Sis... is a police officer?"

Madoka was a little surprised to hear that. Sure, her father was a police officer, but her sister saw how busy he always was and how he never got time off, so she always said she wasn't interested in joining the police force.
But, somehow... seeing Tomoe as a police officer felt appropriate.
Plus, as a female officer in the youth division... She could provide encouragement for young boys and girls, just like she used to...
"...Hehe.@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1120. Ahahahah, ahaha... ha..."
How funny. It was somehow amusing and nostalgic... She began to cry, and couldn't stop.
Yamaoki gazed at Madoka with a gentle look in his eyes...
After that, Yamaoki visited the club at least once a week and always requested Madoka... and every time, he'd give her an update about Tomoe.
During those meetings she came to learn that Tomoe was apparently someone he trusted enough to confide in. And as Tomoe's superior, Madoka was able to inquire with him about the details of her work.
Then, she gradually started meeting up with him outside of work, to eat and chat...
Madoka felt sincerity in Yamaoki unlike any other man she'd ever met, and began taking more of an interest in him.
And, one day.
"...Huh?@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1122. A... patrol officer?"
"Yes, that's right.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1265. Actually, I was just transferred to the position of police chief."

"It's a place in Aichi prefecture called Kakiuchi City... It's a quiet town with robust transportation, and it's a much easier place to live than your current area.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1267. If you'd like... I was wondering if maybe you'd consider coming with me."
"Hee hee... is this a proposal, then?"
"Oh, no... it's not like that..."

Feeling embarrassed, Yamaoki drank his whiskey on the rocks in one gulp... and it must've gotten caught in his throat, because he started coughing. Seeing that, Madoka had a hard time holding back a burst of laughter.

...Madoka understood that Yamaoki was concerned about their age difference. For him, she was the daughter of a former subordinate, so when they walked side by side they looked more like a parent and child rather than a pair of lovers.
He was awkward, and yet also reliable from time to time. ...No matter what the world had to say, deep in Madoka's heart she had already decided.
"But... then how does that relate to me being a police officer?@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1125. I'd be happy to just be a stay-at-home wife, or a housekeeper."
"...Madoka-kun, what do you think of Tomoe-kun?"
"What do I think... of Sis?"
There was no need to even ask... and the question seemed somehow unrelated to the topic at hand, so Madoka quizzically tilted her head.
She loved her sister. She respected her. Even though they argued and had a falling out, she considered her an ideal woman, even now.
But... on the other hand, Madoka...
"Of course I like her.@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1128. ...I'm not sure what she's thinking...@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1129. but the truth is... I respect my sister more than anyone else in the world."
"Hmm... I thought so."
"I realize this may be rash of me to say, but...@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1272. it seems like you feel a strong inferiority complex when comparing yourself with Tomoe-kun."
"Could that be the reason you're not getting along well with her...?"
"Th-that's not..."
"That doesn't mean you're doing anything wrong by your sister."
"She was blessed with exceptional talent, so it's natural to think yourself inferior... but that way of thinking is a slippery slope that will take over all your thoughts."
Madoka couldn't say anything otherwise... so she silently poured some more whiskey into Yamaoki's empty glass. ...It was just as Yamaoki said, so she couldn't laugh it off or gloss over it.

Her sister was exceptionally well studied, and had a pleasant personality. ...But Madoka, on the other hand, had no strong education or any meaningful merits in her background. Even though she tried her best in her own erratic way, she never accomplished anything.
(Even though we were born from the same parents... how did we end up so different?)
The absurdity and injustice of it all was frustrating... so at some point, Madoka stopped bothering to try her best, and even started to hate it.
No matter how hard she tried, she'd never surpass her sister. Once she recognized that fact, she fully realized that she simply lacked the capability.
...Rather than enduring that pain, it's far easier if you simply don't bother trying hard from the start.
The truth is, she could do it. But telling herself that killed her motivation...
(I know I'm pathetic.@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1135. ...I understand that.)
Nevertheless, any time she stood beside her sister she started to feel distant... it was a vicious cycle of ever increasing self-loathing.
Allowing Tomoe to go on living her life freely... was really just an excuse.
She just didn't want to hate herself and her beloved sister by demonstrating the growing difference between the two of them...

"I wish... I wish there was at least one thing I could be better than my sister at.@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1137. I'm very proud to have such an exceptional sister... but sometimes it's a heavy burden."
"I understand.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1277. But... I was thinking about that, and I felt it would be good for you to try doing something together with your sister."
Madoka tilted her head, unable to grasp what Yamaoki was getting at.

She left her sister behind because she didn't want the miserable experience of constantly being compared with her. And yet, he'd suggest she go into the same profession as her and compete against her abilities...?
"Kaoru-san... is this a joke?@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1140. If this is a prank it's pretty upsetting, y'know?"

"Ha ha ha.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1279. I'm not kidding. I'm completely serious.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1280. I thought this would be the best way for you to learn to get over your inferiority complex."

"...But the competition would be over before it even started.@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1142. Sis was already selected for that exceptional group, and I'd just be starting out as a regular officer.@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1143. The speed and quality of our work would be completely different... and that would just make me feel miserable."
"Really... I wonder about that?@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1282. Are you only looking at Tomoe-kun's accomplishments when you say that?"

"Tomoe-kun has actually made plenty of mistakes.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1284. She's experienced more than her fair share of conflicts and frustrations, and she's probably invoked the anger of her bosses and people around her more than anyone else."
"She's clumsy, stubborn, naive, and imprudent.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1286. ...That's my assessment of her."
"Hold on... Kaoru-san?@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1146. Why are you saying this...?!"
Madoka glared at Yamaoki as she reproached him, upset that he'd say something so awful.
...But the look on his face showed no malice or aversion toward Tomoe. In fact, there was even heartwarming kindness in there.
"Right now... what's the difference between you and Tomoe?@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1288. Is it talent?@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1289. Or is it your environment?"
"Well... maybe it's both?"

"No, that's not true.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1291. And I think the best way to prove that is to stand right beside her.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1292. Is it really talent and the environment that got your sister into her current position...? Let's take a closer look."
"...I'll use my leverage and speak with HR about opening a position for a patrol officer.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1294. Whatever you do after that is up to you."
"And then, I took the police recruitment test here... and was instated as an officer."

"Then I told you about it and we went out to celebrate.@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1151. ...And by the way, I don't need to remind you that was when I confessed to you about Kaoru-san and we got into a fight, do I?"
"...Right."
At last, the doubts Tomoe had been holding back all this time finally thawed, and she was convinced.
She knew perfectly well that Madoka didn't like their father's job as a police officer.

Days off weren't just infrequent, they were practically nonexistent. And sometimes he'd get injured bad enough to need hospitalization... so even when Madoka was young, she never had a positive perception of being a police officer.
But despite that, she became an officer anyway... and that was the reason why.
"But... when I became a police officer just like you, I understood.@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1153. Sis, you really are great."
"That's not some vague assessment coming from an inferiority complex telling me I'll never match up to you.@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1155. You confronted your failures and reflected on them..."
"You agonized about every next move... but you still continued trying to step forward.@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1157. That's the big difference between the two of us."
Madoka had the attitude that if she failed... she'd just give up. But Tomoe would stand back up and keep trying again and again until she succeeded.
She simply had the courage, the persistence, and the dedication to do so.
Of course, there may also be some underlying talent beneath that. ...But the biggest gulf between them was how seriously Tomoe applied herself.
"Sis, I've always felt that you were better than me.@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1159. I convinced myself that I'd never be as good as you no matter what I did, so I just gave up."

"Up until this point, I told myself no matter how hard I tried, no matter how much I wished for it, it was all useless.@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1161. I couldn't face myself and consider the possibilities."
"...But by doing that, I wasn't valuing myself."
"...That's not true."

"That's why I decided... from now on, I'll try to face myself more seriously.@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1164. Without laughing off the necessary effort, and without being afraid of frustrations or failure."
"And by doing that, I won't have to compare myself to you or anyone else.@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1166. I'll just think about whether or not I made my personal best effort. That's what matters to me."
"Madoka..."

"Well, I'm still not as bold as you are, Sis, so I might stumble along the way.@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1168. ...But I'll keep on trying until it works out.@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1169. That's what I've decided."
Learning how her sister felt... Tomoe suddenly felt an overwhelming rush of emotion.
She delighted just to hear her say that... and she was happy that she had come to such a heartfelt decision.
...However.
It wasn't just that... That wasn't all she worked hard for.
"Me too... Madoka.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2619. I don't think I could have made it where I am without you."
"...Huh?@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1171. ...Me?"

"We're in the same boat.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2621. To me, you were always my precious little sister.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2622. So, I could do anything as long as it was for you... That's what kept me going."
Tomoe also knew how much Madoka respected her. That's why she hated betraying her beloved sister's expectations.
There were so many times she endured hardship and wanted to give up.
Countless times where she considered that it might be better if she yielded.
...However.
She always asked herself... If I gave up here, what would Madoka think?
She'd be sad. Discouraged. ...Just imagining her face brought Tomoe dread.
So, if she didn't have Madoka,
If she didn't have a little sister who always believed she was amazing... she could never have come as far as she did.
Those were undoubtedly Tomoe's true feelings...

"...You probably didn't realize it, but you were supporting me all this time.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2624. So thank you, Madoka.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2625. And, from now on... let's make the best of it."

"...@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2627.A-anyway, that's enough chitchat.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2628. We need to get these files packed up quickly. The DPO special investigation department will be here soon, won't they?"
"Yeah, that's right."
After saying that, they resumed packing the documents.
In this room they had the drug analysis results prepared by Souma and the ledgers and materials seized from Tsukada's home, which they had been working all night to prepare copies of.
The originals were to be kept at joint investigation HQ, packed away in the records room behind the conference room.
"Umm, there's nothing else we're forgetting. So, all that's left is..."

Tomoe muttered that to herself, while opening the drawer to the Assistant Director's cabinet. Then, she looked inside to see if anything was forgotten... and she suddenly stopped on one particular file.
"Oh, this..."
It was a file full of carefully separated notebook pages stored in individual plastic bags. ...These were the investigation records that Yuusuke Minai left behind before his death.
(In the end... it was a dead end.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2632. I'm sorry, Dad...)
Regrets hounded Tomoe as she opened the file her father left behind. On the last page, the text "Kakiuchi Airport, June, Wednesday. Go." was written in black ink.
"Huh?@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1175. What's that note?"
"Hmm?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2634. Oh, uhh, here..."
"Oh, this is Dad's handwriting."
"Kakiuchi Airport, June, Wednesday... C1?@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1178. Or maybe CI?"
As Madoka looked at the file from the side, those words leaked out.
Her nonchalant voice pierced Tomoe's ears and stimulated her eardrums... and then Tomoe's consciousness wavered, as if time itself had stopped.
"H-hold on...?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2637. This... can be read as C1?!"
"Yeah, he always had a habit of drawing Cs this way, y'know?@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1180. Dad was always pretty terrible at writing the English alphabet~."
"Th-then... what about this part?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2639. I thought it was a "け" written in hiragana, but..."
"Hmm... LT?@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1182. Or maybe LX...?@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1183. Doesn't it look that way?"

Tomoe froze on the spot like she had been struck by a bolt of lightning, then looked through the notes. ...After hearing it, yes, this really did look like her father's poor attempt at writing alphabetic characters.
(Yes... that's it...!)
She had always assumed it was written in hiragana "いけ" which likely meant "go" and she was desperately trying to think of what he might've meant by that.
But now, she finally realized that her perspective was far too narrow. That she had stopped thinking.
And then... then!!
What do they use alphabetic letters to denote at Kakiuchi airport...?!
"Could these be... letter codes?!"
"Huh?@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1185. What's that...?"
"Letter codes are alphabetic shorthand representations of airport and airline names."
"A two-letter code generally refers to an airline name.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2645. And "C1"... is Central First Airlines!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2646. And "LX" is Loberta Airlines...!"
"Wow, good going sis. You always did want to become a stewardess.@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1187. You're so well informed~."
But she could no longer even hear Madoka's teasing.
And then, when she noticed Fujita coming back after buying a couple cans of juice, Tomoe rushed at him seemingly in a fit of anger.
"Fujita-kun, hold up!"
"Wha?@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1155. What is it, Minai-san??"

"Please...!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2649. I need you to do some investigation into Central First Airlines and Loberta Airlines, ASAP!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2650. I need records for the past 20 years... no, the past 30 years of activity!"

"Wh-what's gotten into you all of a sudden?@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1189. Plus, Loberta Airlines doesn't even exist anymore, you know?@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1190. I saw it on the news a while back. They merged with some other airline."
"...?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2652. Who did they merge with?!"
"Oh, I know the answer to that.@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1157. They merged with Central First Airlines, and now they're just known as Central Airlines."
"Central... Airlines?!"
That name... was something she'd heard recently. ...But where? Where on earth did she hear it...?!
Tomoe wracked her brain, desperately trying to remember the significance of that name.
And that moment...
"Fujita-kun... when was this merger?"

"Uhh... it happened when I was studying for entrance exams, so probably about 12 years ago.@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1159. I'm pretty sure I was reading my study books when I heard about it on the radio, so I think that's right."
"You have a good memory, Fujita-kun.@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1192. That's not exactly a famous airline, is it?"

"Well actually, I'm a pretty big fan of their air shows.@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1161. Central Airlines handles transportation of the equipment used by aerial acrobatics teams in the west.@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1162. I'm sure they're even listed as supporters on the brochure."
"I see.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2656. And they hold an air show every June at the Kakiuchi airport...?!"
...It connected.
Connection, after connection, after connection... It was all connected!!
"Madoka... Fujita-kun...!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2658. Ahahahaha, aahahahahahah!!"
"Eh?!@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1164. M-Minai-san??"
"H-hold on, Sis!@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1194. What's gotten into you??"
"You guys are the best... The best!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2660. Aahahahahahahah!!!"
She hugged her two bewildered onlookers while laughing at the top of her lungs for the first time in a long time. Madoka and Fujita just nervously looked down at her as she did.
Just then... the phone began to ring.
By the second ring, Madoka realized there was nobody else around, so she broke out of Tomoe's embrace and went to answer the phone.
"Hello, you've reached the First Investigation Division.@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1196. ...Yes?"
"What's going on?"
"Sis, you have a visitor.@k@vS14/29/VT4Bb_madoka1198. It's someone from the special investigation department at the Tokyo District Prosecutor's Office..."
